claim: Exit-during-attach-window swallows the pane's final output bytes: pty:data arriving while an attach hold window is open is pushed to rec.held; if the pane then exits before the attach res, paneExited() delivers the pty:exit evt but the held bytes are silently discarded (release() finds panes.get(id) !== rec and clears held) and the failed attach carries no snapshot either - the subscribed client loses the pane's last output forever, violating REQ-010's data-before-exit contract for exactly this race.
fix: In paneExited(), flush rec.held as pty:data frames (bound + subscribed guarded) BEFORE engine.markExit/the exit evt, then clear held - final data precedes final status precedes exit, and later release() calls find held empty.
resolution: Fixed in src/agent/session-store.ts paneExited(): held window bytes are flushed as pty:data (bound+subscribed guarded) BEFORE engine.markExit and the pty:exit evt, then held is cleared so later release() calls find it empty. Verified by the full agent suite green (the exit-ordering vectors TEST-1904/TEST-771 still pin status-before-exit).

claim: feed('') issues term.write('', cb) and bumps issuedSeq; if xterm ever short-circuits zero-length writes without invoking the callback, parsedSeq never reaches issuedSeq and every later snapshot() waits forever (a wedged attach). No current backend emits empty chunks, but the barrier's liveness should not depend on an undocumented xterm edge.
fix: Guard feed(): return without issuing a write (and without bumping issuedSeq) when data.length === 0.
resolution: Fixed in src/agent/replay.ts feed(): zero-length data returns without issuing a write or bumping issuedSeq - the barrier can no longer depend on xterm's empty-write callback behavior. TEST-1901 barrier vectors green.

claim: The async attach continuation uses .then(onOk, onErr); a throw INSIDE onOk (e.g. init.send throwing on the res frame in an embedding that does not wrap send) yields a rejected promise with no downstream handler - an unhandledRejection crash vector in a long-lived agent process.
fix: Chain a terminal .catch that diags (bounded) and never rethrows; the reply guard already ensures no double-res.
resolution: Fixed in src/agent/session.ts: the attach continuation is a .then(...).catch(...) chain whose terminal catch diags (bounded) and attempts the internal res inside its own try/catch backstop - no throw path can surface as an unhandledRejection. Superseded in part by FINDING-007's try/finally which guarantees drain on a throwing res send.

claim: bind() on a destroyed store is deliberately allowed (empty-inventory contract), but spawn() on a destroyed store then creates a REAL pty pane inside a store whose destroy() already ran and is idempotent-no-op - an unkillable zombie process leak if a runtime ever races destroy() against a live connection's spawn.
fix: spawn() returns { ok: false } (spawn-failed with an actionable message) when destroyed; write/resize/kill/attach already degrade safely via the empty pane map.
resolution: Fixed in src/agent/session-store.ts spawn(): a destroyed store rejects spawns with an actionable spawn-failed message - no pane can be created that destroy() will never kill. write/resize/kill/attach already degraded safely via the empty pane map.

claim: PaneRec.replay is initialized with a lie-typed placeholder (null as unknown as PaneReplay) and patched after replayFactory(); the intermediate rec is reachable by the engine's synchronous register callback while carrying an invalid replay field, and the unwind needs an extra catch arm.
fix: Call replayFactory() BEFORE inserting the rec (a throw then unwinds nothing), assign the real replay in the initializer, and drop the placeholder + first unwind path.
resolution: Fixed in src/agent/session-store.ts spawn(): replayFactory() is invoked BEFORE the pane record exists (a throw unwinds nothing) and the record initializer carries the real replay - the null-cast placeholder and its extra unwind arm are gone.

claim: Overlapping pty:attach on one pane double-delivers window bytes: with the barrier captured at dispatch, a byte held for attach A's window that arrives before attach B's dispatch is INSIDE B's snapshot, yet the shared rec.held array still flushes it after B's res (A's release skips flushing at holds>0; B's drain flushes everything) - B's client applies the byte twice (snapshot + event). The code comment claiming the final repaint stays exact is wrong for this interleaving.
fix: v1 serializes attach windows per pane by REJECTION: attach while rec.holds > 0 fails fast with code internal and an actionable retry message (sequential re-attach - the spec'd surface - is unaffected; a same-chunk pipelined double-attach gets a deterministic error instead of corruption). The now-vestigial per-pane attachChain is removed.
resolution: Fixed in src/agent/session-store.ts attach(): overlap is rejected deterministically (holds > 0 -> internal with a retry-after-res message); attach windows on a pane never coexist so no byte can ride both a snapshot and an event; the vestigial per-pane attachChain was removed. Sequential re-attach (TEST-1906) and the pipelined-single-attach ordering vector stay green; the incorrect final-repaint-exactness comment was removed.

claim: A throwing res send skips outcome.drain(): reply() sets replied=true before init.send throws, so the terminal catch's fail() no-ops, drain is never called, and with the store still bound the pane's holds stays >0 - every subsequent pty:data for that pane is buffered into held forever (silent data wedge) instead of flowing or detaching.
fix: Send the res inside try { ok(outcome.result) } finally { outcome.drain() }: on a throwing send, drain's own deliver() path detects the broken sink (store unbinds + onSendFailure ends the connection), and holds are released either way.
resolution: Fixed in src/agent/session.ts: the res send runs inside try { ok(result) } finally { outcome.drain() } - a throwing send still releases the hold window, and the drain's deliver path detects the broken sink (store unbinds, onSendFailure ends the connection with exit 1). TEST-1905's throwing-send vector green.

claim: A resize processed inside the attach window (e.g. attach+resize pipelined in one stdin chunk) yields result cols/rows read at res time with a snapshot serialized at the barrier - the serialized content can rewrap when written at the reported dims. Not corruption (serialize output is dimension-free escape text; the pane self-heals on next output; F21 resizes after attach anyway) but the contract should be explicit.
fix: Document on AgentAttachResult and in remote-agent.md: cols/rows are the CURRENT authoritative pty dims at res time; the snapshot repaints content and may rewrap if a resize landed inside the attach window - clients size the terminal from cols/rows, then write the snapshot.
resolution: Documented (behavior deliberately unchanged): AgentAttachResult's doc comment (src/agent/session-api.ts) and docs/features/remote-agent.md now state cols/rows are the current authoritative pty dims at res time, that a resize inside the window can only rewrap (serialize output is dimension-free), and that clients size from dims then write the snapshot (F21 resizes after attach).

claim: rec.holds++ precedes rec.replay.snapshot(); a SYNCHRONOUS snapshot throw (the real replay serializes synchronously on the already-parsed fast path - an xterm serialize fault throws here) escapes attach() before the release closure exists: the session's dispatch catch answers internal, but the pane is left with holds>0 forever and subscribed data black-holes into held.
fix: Wrap the barrier capture in try/catch: on a synchronous throw, undo the hold (holds = max(0, holds-1)) and resolve { ok:false, internal } with the bounded cause.
resolution: Fixed in src/agent/session-store.ts attach(): the barrier capture is wrapped in try/catch - a synchronous snapshot()/serialize throw undoes the hold (holds = max(0, holds-1)) and resolves an internal outcome with the bounded cause, so subscribed data can never black-hole into held.

claim: Frozen test files were modified during implementation: frozen tests changed: tests/agent-session.test.ts (modified), tests/remote-protocol-capabilities.test.ts (modified)
fix: Revert the test changes. Tests are frozen after phase 4. If a test is genuinely wrong, route the issue back to phase 4 (Tests) instead of editing tests during implementation.
resolution: Adjudicated as a batch-merge freeze-manifest artifact, not an integrity violation: the two 'modified' frozen files (tests/agent-session.test.ts, tests/remote-protocol-capabilities.test.ts) are F17's OWN tests-phase-sanctioned versions arriving via the 'merge main into orky/F18' weld commit 76a298f; F18's freeze manifest simply predates F17's merge. Mechanical evidence: git diff --diff-filter=M main..HEAD -- tests/ is EMPTY (merged tests/ is exactly main's tests/ plus F18's 7 new files); full combined suite green 249 files / 1767 tests / build exit 0. Expected ADR-031 post-merge re-gate condition.
resolvedBy: Kevin (delegated: full-auto app-run mandate; adjudicated by orchestrator on mechanical evidence, surfaced at batch boundary for veto)
